support_note: High-throughput clinical variant is the primary reference for scanner capacity sizing — the P480 class at 480 slides per batch is the canonical anchor for this scenario.
High-throughput clinical variant includes AI tissue detection and safety container, which directly implement the QC and rescan handling scenario.

High-throughput clinical variant supports the capability dimension of multi-scanner load balancing (multi-layer, polarization) but modeling a full multi-scanner fleet requires the multi-scanner-lab variant instead.
Medium-throughput research variant serves capacity sizing for smaller labs where a full 480-slide scanner is overkill.
Medium-throughput research variant can support upstream workflow integration for smaller labs where the scanner is not the bottleneck.
Multi-scanner lab variant is the canonical form of the load balancing scenario — N scanners of mixed capability with LIS-driven routing.
End-to-end lab variant is the canonical form of the upstream workflow integration scenario — it explicitly models tissue processing, embedding, cutting, and staining upstream of the scanner.
